pipeline_environment_quirks.overview: A handful of facts about how this Jenkins + Bob pipeline is wired aren't inferable from the repo. Stages will silently break if they're written without these in mind. This rules file is the source of truth for those facts; they apply to *every* stage that touches git, the workspace, or files Bob reads.

quirk.name: git_safe_directory
quirk.title: safe.directory must be configured before any git command
quirk.description: The build-tools (maven) container runs as a UID different from the one that owns the files Jenkins's checkout step writes. Without an explicit trust configuration, git refuses to operate with: "fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/workspace/...'"
quirk.fix: // As the FIRST line of any shell step that calls git sh ''' git config --global --add safe.directory "$WORKSPACE" git diff origin/main...HEAD > git-diff.txt '''
quirk.why_workspace_not_wildcard: Use $WORKSPACE — Jenkins-provided env var pointing at this job's workspace — rather than the '*' wildcard. Scoping the trust to the actual job directory is safer than trusting every directory the container happens to see.

quirk.name: bob_cwd_subtree_only
quirk.title: Bob's tooling only reads files within its working directory subtree
quirk.description: When askBob invokes Bob in container('bob'), Bob's working directory is the Jenkins job workspace — physically /workspace/workspace/<folder>/<job>/. Bob's file-reading tools refuse paths *outside* that subtree. The volume mount root /workspace/ is technically reachable from the container's filesystem but is *outside* Bob's reachable subtree, so absolute paths starting /workspace/... will fail.
quirk.implication: Any file shared between a shell step (which Jenkins writes via writeFile or shell redirect) and a Bob prompt (which tells Bob "read this file") must use a relative path on both sides. Relative paths resolve to $WORKSPACE in the shell step and to Bob's CWD (the same physical directory) in the Bob prompt — same file, both sides happy.
quirk.error_signature: "git-diff.txt is not accessible from the current workspace directory" — or similar phrasing about path access. If you see this, you wrote to (or asked Bob to read) an absolute /workspace/... path. Switch to relative.

quirk.name: three_dot_diff
quirk.title: Use three-dot diff syntax for PR-style diffs
quirk.description: git diff origin/main...HEAD (three dots) gives the diff of "everything this branch added since it forked from main" — the same view a reviewer sees in a PR. git diff origin/main..HEAD (two dots) compares two endpoints and includes commits that have landed on main since the branch forked, which is noisy and confusing.
quirk.bad: git diff origin/main..HEAD (two dots — wrong shape)

quirk.name: checkout_scm_already_fetched_main
quirk.title: checkout scm already fetches origin/main
quirk.description: Jenkins's `checkout scm` step (run once at the top of the pipeline) fetches origin/main alongside the working branch. Stages don't need to add an explicit `git fetch origin main` before diffing.
quirk.fix: Just diff against origin/main directly; don't add a redundant fetch.

quirk.name: default_container_is_build_tools
quirk.title: Default container for unwrapped sh steps is build-tools
quirk.description: The Jenkinsfile sets `defaultContainer 'build-tools'`. Any sh step not wrapped in a container() block runs in build-tools (maven:3.9 + eclipse-temurin-17 + git). You don't need to wrap mvn or git steps.
quirk.relevance: You only switch containers for the bob CLI (handled by askBob) or for oc / kubectl (wrap in container('oc-tools')). See 3_container_selection.xml.

reusable_environment_block.description: When asking Bob to *write* a pipeline stage, prepend the following block to your prompt. It captures every quirk above in a form Bob can use directly. Same block, every pipeline lab.
reusable_environment_block.block:
Environment context for our Jenkins + Bob pipeline setup:
- As the FIRST line of any git-using shell step, run: git config --global --add safe.directory "$WORKSPACE" Without this, git refuses to operate inside the build-tools container with "fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository". Scope to $WORKSPACE, not '*'.
- Bob's working directory is the Jenkins job workspace, and Bob's tooling only reads files within that subtree. Any file shared between a shell step and Bob must use a PLAIN RELATIVE PATH (e.g. `git-diff.txt`), NEVER `/workspace/...`.
- Use three-dot syntax for PR-style diffs: git diff origin/main...HEAD.
- Jenkins's `checkout scm` already fetches origin/main — no extra fetch needed.
- Default container for sh steps is build-tools (Maven + JDK 17 + git). Wrap steps in container('oc-tools') for oc/kubectl. Bob calls go through askBob.

quick_reference.command: git config --global --add safe.directory "$WORKSPACE" # first line of any git step
git diff origin/main...HEAD > git-diff.txt # three-dot, relative path
askBob("Read git-diff.txt and ...", 'mode-slug') # Bob reads same relative path
